title: Signal Theory — Universal Signal Detection Framework
description: Signal detection tools grounded in 6 axioms and 5 theorems. Includes threshold-based detection, SDT decision matrix (d-prime, sensitivity, specificity, MCC), conservation law verification, multi-stage pipelines, cascading severity levels, and parallel detector fusion. Pure Rust via nexcore-signal-theory.

tools.name: get-axioms
tools.description: List all 6 axioms of signal theory with their primitive mappings. A1 Data Generation (ν), A2 Noise Dominance (∅), A3 Signal Existence (∃), A4 Boundary Requirement (∂), A5 Disproportionality (κ), A6 Causal Inference (→).

tools.name: get-theorems
tools.description: List all 5 theorems of signal theory with their prerequisites and formal statements.

tools.name: detect
tools.description: Run signal detection: compare observed count vs expected count against a threshold. Returns ratio, difference, detection outcome, and signal strength level.

tools.name: decision-matrix
tools.description: Compute SDT decision matrix metrics from a 2×2 classification table (hits, misses, false alarms, correct rejections). Returns sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, accuracy, F1, MCC, d-prime, and response bias.

tools.name: conservation-check
tools.description: Verify conservation laws (L1-L4) on a decision matrix. Checks total count conservation and information conservation (d-prime bounds).

parameters.name: max_dprime
parameters.description: Maximum d-prime for L4 information conservation check

tools.name: pipeline
tools.description: Run a multi-stage signal detection pipeline. A value passes through sequential stages, each with its own threshold. Reports per-stage pass/fail and overall verdict.

parameters.name: stages
parameters.description: Array of {name, phase, threshold} stage definitions

tools.name: cascade
tools.description: Cascading threshold evaluation — find the highest severity level exceeded. Thresholds in ascending order with labels. Reports highest level breached.

tools.name: parallel
tools.description: Parallel signal detection across two independent detectors. Mode 'both' (AND) requires both to fire. Mode 'either' (OR) requires at least one.
